title: Saudi OT Cybersecurity Platform
entry_mechanism: Cyberani (Aramco Digital) commercial spin-out with PSAP-supported international OT security company JV
executive_summary:
Operational Technology (OT) cybersecurity — protecting the industrial control systems, SCADA platforms, and PLCs that run Saudi Arabia's oil refineries, desalination plants, power grid, and petrochemical facilities — is a critical national security requirement. The 2012 Shamoon attack and subsequent incidents have demonstrated that Saudi Arabia's critical infrastructure is a persistent target for sophisticated adversaries. Today, OT cybersecurity is provided primarily by foreign vendors (Claroty, Dragos, Nozomi Networks) whose products process data about Saudi critical infrastructure on foreign servers.

Node DG-3 targets a Saudi-developed OT cybersecurity platform: asset discovery, network monitoring, threat detection, and incident response tools specifically designed for the OT environments of Saudi Arabia's critical infrastructure sectors. The platform must operate on air-gapped Saudi infrastructure, with Saudi threat intelligence integrated from Aramco and NCA data.

The global OT cybersecurity market was valued at $20.7 billion in 2024 growing at 23.4% CAGR. Saudi Arabia's critical infrastructure — one of the world's most concentrated — creates a massive domestic market. A Saudi OT cybersecurity platform that proves itself on Aramco and SWCC (water) infrastructure has immediate GCC and MENA export potential.

companies.name: Claroty
companies.rationale: Claroty is the OT security market leader. A technology licensing arrangement for a Saudi-built OT platform is commercially viable.

companies.name: Dragos
companies.rationale: Dragos provides ICS-specific threat intelligence. Saudi deployment with Saudi threat intelligence integration.

companies.name: Nozomi Networks
companies.rationale: Nozomi provides OT asset visibility and threat detection. Technology licensing for Saudi-operated platform.

companies.name: Schneider Electric (Cybersecurity)
companies.rationale: Schneider supplies OT systems to Saudi Arabia's critical infrastructure and has OT cybersecurity capability. Non-US European technology.

companies.name: Siemens Energy (OT Security)
companies.rationale: Siemens Energy has OT security for power generation applicable to Saudi SEC and ARAMCO power assets.

partners.name: Cyberani (Aramco Digital subsidiary)
partners.current: OT cybersecurity for Aramco infrastructure; proprietary Aramco threat intelligence; deployed across Aramco's industrial control network
partners.upgrade: Commercialise Cyberani OT platform; Claroty technology licensing; NCA certification for critical infrastructure; expand to SWCC, SEC, SABIC, and Maaden as national critical infrastructure customers

partners.name: National Cybersecurity Authority
partners.current: OT security standards; critical infrastructure protection
partners.upgrade: NCA mandatory use policy for Saudi OT cybersecurity platform across critical infrastructure; Saudi OT threat intelligence sharing framework; NCA certification for Saudi OT platform

partners.name: KACST / Cybersecurity Research
partners.current: Cybersecurity research; some OT protocol research
partners.upgrade: GADD-KACST OT security research programme; Saudi-specific industrial protocol analysis; ICS attack simulation for Saudi critical infrastructure

partners.name: Saudi Electricity Company (SEC)
partners.current: Power grid OT systems; limited OT cybersecurity; foreign vendor dependent
partners.upgrade: SEC as anchor customer for Saudi OT platform; SEC-Cyberani deployment; Saudi power grid threat intelligence contribution to national OT threat database
